comment: Machine-readable mirror of docs/risk-register.md. Read by scripts/production-readiness.mjs. Open + P0 → red row in the aggregator; open + P1 → yellow; mitigating + P0 → yellow; closed → not surfaced. When this file changes, update the .md mirror in the same PR.

risks.title: External smart-contract audit not yet complete (BrainEscrow)
risks.mitigation_summary: Mainnet deploy is boot-fenced: composition/escrow-audit-gate.ts refuses to start the api when BRAIN_ESCROW_ADDRESS is set on any non-testnet chain unless the committed audit record is approved for that chain and the operator sets BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_RECEIPT or BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_APPROVED="true". API boot checks explicit BASE_RPC_URL with eth_chainId and refuses to start when it differs from BRAIN_BASE_CHAIN_ID. CI guard check-escrow-audit-marker catches the same misconfig at PR-review time before the boot fence has to fire. The deployed on-chain bytecode is verified at mainnet boot against the audited immutable-masked runtime hash via eth_getCode (assertDeployedEscrowBytecode), and audit-build evidence is recomputed from the working tree in CI (verify-audited-build.mjs), so a contract or toolchain change cannot ride an older approval.
exit_criteria: external audit completes with no critical or high findings
audited commit SHA + tag pinned in contracts/AUDIT-SCOPE.md
deployed mainnet bytecode verified to match audited bytecode (control shipped: eth_getCode boot fence; pending live mainnet confirmation)
BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_RECEIPT set to the audit report URL/hash (or legacy BRAIN_ESCROW_AUDIT_APPROVED="true") in production env

risks.title: Tenant blob purge not implemented (GDPR Article 17 gap)
risks.mitigation_summary: RFC 0003 phase B shipped: tenant deletion enqueues a durable tenant_blob_purge_jobs row in the deletion transaction; a privileged worker (blob-purge-worker.ts) drains it via BlobAdapter.purgeTenant with bounded backoff, a dead-letter state, and version-aware permanent erasure (S3 all object versions + delete markers; Azure versions + snapshots), restricted to the worker by a CI call-site guard (check-blob-purge-callsite). 2026-06-07 review hardening shipped: (i) crash-safe lease recovery (stale 'purging' jobs reclaimed past a lease timeout; every status write fenced on a unique lock token, so a crashed worker cannot strand a job); (ii) structured failure classification (transient / authorization / unknown errors are retried, only a confirmed WORM / object-lock is the terminal blocked_legal_hold, so a 503 no longer masquerades as a legal hold); (iii) a transactional audit outbox (each state transition writes its audit intent in the same transaction and a publisher delivers it idempotently, removing the emit-before-write orphan and the 'audit-emit-failed' sentinel). An end-to-end erasure test now drives runBlobPurgeCycle against a real MemoryBlobAdapter (not a mocked purgeTenant), asserting the deleted tenant's bytes are erased and a co-resident tenant's survive. Remaining: a production-shaped integration test against live S3/Azure (localstack-in-CI) proving permanent erasure on the deployed adapters.
exit_criteria: tenant_blob_purge_jobs migration
BlobAdapter.purge() on memory/s3/azure
tenant-blob-purge-worker.ts
check-blob-purge-callsites.mjs lint guard
integration test proving end-to-end erasure

risks.title: Staging VM deploy chain not yet exercised
risks.mitigation_summary: All safety controls (boot fences, gate, RLS) are deploy-substrate-independent. Disclosure explicit in architecture/enterprise-readiness.md. docs/r03-staging-deploy-runbook.md gives the ordered VM path: build one image, deploy staging on green main, smoke the staging commit, then require GitHub production environment approval before production promote. Closing staging VM provisioning, GitHub secrets, host env files, and the production environment reviewer gate is the remaining operator work.
exit_criteria: staging VM secrets provisioned
build deploy staging-validation chain runs cleanly end-to-end
production deploy logs show all 5 boot fences armed via brain.runtime.capabilities
rollback procedure exercised against staging

risks.title: Full money-movement E2E not yet enforced in CI
risks.mitigation_summary: A money_movement_e2e job now runs on EVERY PR (.github/workflows/pr.yml) against a real pg+redis stack + booted demo API: the full chain provision->ingest->normalize->propose->policy->execute, plus assertions that the §6 gate ran checks 8/9.5/11.5 (none not_applicable, read from the proof's gate_checks) and that three negatives are actively rejected (duplicate payment, excessive-amount policy reject, missing-approval). audit-before->after linkage is now asserted in strict mode (the executed PI's proof must contain payment_intent.execute.before preceding payment_intent.execute.after, with the after event chained via prev_event_hash). Remaining: localstack-in-CI for the blob path (EC1) and full strict-proof (on-chain merkle anchor) mode, which needs a CI-available Base RPC; receivable-direction / low-confidence negatives are layerable follow-ups.
exit_criteria: deterministic local-stack-in-CI (pg+redis+localstack+boot binary in GH Actions)
golden-path demo runs in strict mode on every PR
any §6 check 8 / 9.5 / 11.5 going not_applicable fails the build
audit-before → audit-after linkage asserted

risks.title: Rail catalog vs docs drift
risks.mitigation_summary: check-rails-catalog-drift CI guard wired into pnpm run lint compares both files; drift is now structurally impossible at PR merge time.

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount session-key caps are unit-blind to ERC20 decimals
risks.mitigation_summary: Closed in code: SessionKey now carries an explicit capToken field. In ERC20 mode (capToken != 0), caps are denominated in capToken's raw units; grantSessionKey enforces allowedTargets == [capToken] and selector subset constraints; executeViaSessionKey verifies target == capToken AND value == 0. Caps are always meterable in the token's native units. Foundry tests test_R06_usdc6dp_capEnforcesInTokenUnits + test_R06_dai18dp_capEnforcesInTokenUnits + test_R06_execute_rejectsValueInErc20Mode + test_R06_execute_rejectsWrongTargetInErc20Mode prove enforcement. Mainnet enablement still gated on R-01 (external audit).

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount selector decode covers only three ERC20 selectors; non-decodable selectors bypass caps
risks.mitigation_summary: Closed in code: grantSessionKey now rejects any selector outside {transfer, approve, transferFrom} when capToken is set (new error NonDecodableSelectorInErc20Mode). The contract refuses to grant a session key whose caps would silently be bypassed. Foundry test test_R07_grant_rejectsNonDecodableSelectorInErc20Mode proves the rejection; test_R07_grant_rejectsTargetMismatchInErc20Mode catches the related allowlist footgun. Mainnet enablement still gated on R-01.

risks.title: Money-touching rail-adapter test coverage gap
risks.mitigation_summary: Batch 8 P5: error-path coverage added to onchainExecutor (read/write error propagation, receipt-hash sourcing), plaidClient (Plaid API error verbatim), and x402Client (full lifecycle: decimals read, transfer revert, receipt timeout, facilitator 5xx + network error, idempotency-key forwarding, chain selection). PostgresSourceRepository was already at 16-test coverage (reviewer's claim of near-zero was stale). 23 rail-adapter unit tests now pass (was 6). Per-file >=80% line/function/statement coverage gates are now wired into services/api/vitest.config.ts for each money-touching rail adapter (onchainExecutor, plaidClient, x402Client), so a single adapter regressing below 80% fails CI even while the aggregate stays high (verified: forcing onchainExecutor's gate above its real coverage fails the run). The first exit criterion (line-coverage parity wired into test:coverage) is met; the second now has gated scaffolding (R-14): tests/e2e/onchain-executor.testnet.e2e.test.ts drives the real rail against a deployed BrainSmartAccount via the opt-in testnet_onchain_executor_e2e CI job, runnable once testnet fixtures are provisioned (R-03-adjacent).
exit_criteria: Coverage parity with the §6 gate measured at >=80% line coverage on the adapter files (next step: wire coverage gates into test:coverage)
Integration tests on testnet for at least the onchain executor end-to-end (scaffolding shipped in R-14; runs once testnet fixtures/secrets are provisioned)

risks.title: On-chain tumbling vs off-chain rolling spend windows can diverge
risks.mitigation_summary: Batch 8 P3: off-chain agent-window-spend loader switched from a trailing rolling window to a tumbling window using the same floor((ts/period)*period) formula as BrainSmartAccount._windowSpent. Off-chain (agent-budget, check 8.5) and on-chain (session-key budget) now agree at boundaries. The misleading 'mirror' comment in shared/src/gate/gate.ts was corrected. Approve-as-spend concern is structurally NA off-chain (only executed intents are summed; approve never reaches executed). CLOSED: fast-check property tests (services/policy/src/agent-window-spend.windows.test.ts) prove the canonical tumblingWindowStartSeconds equals an independent derivation of the on-chain Solidity integer-division window (ts - ts%p) at T-1/T/T+1, that the off-chain double-precision epoch intermediate does not diverge across the realistic epoch range, and the containment/monotonicity invariants. The remaining criterion (rationalize on-chain approve-as-spend) was explicitly optional and stays deferred (current behavior is conservative).
exit_criteria: Property tests proving off-chain SQL window matches on-chain Solidity window at boundary T = floor(now/period)*period and T-1, T+1
Optional: rationalize approve-as-spend on-chain (defer; current is conservative)

risks.title: BrainSmartAccount NatSpec framing claims 'ERC-4337-style' but no EntryPoint exists
risks.mitigation_summary: BrainSmartAccount.sol contract NatSpec rewritten: now describes the actual design ('Smart account with directly-called session keys. Not ERC-4337: there is no EntryPoint, no UserOperation, no paymaster'). AUDIT-SCOPE.md mirror updated. Surfaced by Opus 4.8 (F-11).

risks.title: Canonical cutover moved the obligation money-path from synchronous to two async workers
risks.mitigation_summary: Phase 5 (RFC 0005) made ledger_obligations/counterparties a projection of canonical: a Merge or document obligation no longer reaches the Ledger synchronously in normalizeFromRaw, but via two scheduled workers in sequence (startCanonicalProjectionWorker raw_parsed->canonical, then startLedgerAparProjectionWorker canonical->Ledger). Closed: (i) an end-to-end integration test drives BOTH worker cycles and asserts an obligation reaches ledger_obligations (the wired path the wedge's synchronous calls did not cover); (ii) both workers emit a records-projected counter AND a lag gauge (brain.canonical.projector.lag_seconds / brain.ledger.apar_projection.lag_seconds = age of the oldest un-projected record); (iii) infra/grafana/projection.json defines two alerts -- ProjectionPipelineStalled (lag>600s with zero throughput) and ProjectionLagHigh (lag>300s) -- so a stalled money-path worker pages instead of silently dropping obligations; (iv) projections are idempotent and replayable, so a recovered worker catches up without data loss; (v) poison-message handling (canonical/0003): a single malformed source record is retried up to maxAttempts (default 5) then quarantined (canonical_projection_log.attempts/quarantined) so it cannot wedge or silently drop a tenant's lane -- it is surfaced via brain.canonical.projector.quarantine.count/.quarantine.depth (and excluded from the lag gauge), and replayQuarantined() drains it after the underlying bug is fixed. The optional in-process liveness probe is unnecessary given the lag-with-zero-throughput alert already detects a wedged worker.

risks.title: Single broad brain_privileged BYPASSRLS role for all cross-tenant API work
risks.mitigation_summary: Three reviews flagged that the API runtime used ONE broad brain_privileged BYPASSRLS role for every cross-tenant background job and resolver, so a confused-deputy bug or compromise in any one privileged path had the cross-tenant power of all of them (raw, canonical, ledger, audit, execution, tenant-deletion). Closed by replacing it with eight least-privilege BYPASSRLS roles (infra/db-roles.sql §4), each granted only its layer's tables: brain_raw_worker (raw_*), brain_canonical_projector (canonical_* + read raw_parsed), brain_ledger_projector (read canonical_*, write only the three ledger projection targets, NOT the money-path ledger_* tables), brain_execution_worker (execution_outbox claim/mark only; the settle re-enters tenant scope on brain_app), brain_audit_verifier (audit_events read + verifier cursor/findings, findings un-erasable), brain_audit_publisher (audit_events read only), brain_resolver (cross-tenant SELECT only), brain_tenant_deletion (GDPR erasure broad DELETE, route-gated; audit_events/anchors preserved). Each connects via its own BRAIN_*_DB_URL (all eight required in production, fenced by db-isolation.ts; dev falls back to DATABASE_URL with a warning). The boot-time assertDbRoles check proves each pool connects as its expected role with a forbidden-privilege list (e.g. the ledger projector cannot INSERT ledger_payment_intents; tenant_deletion cannot DELETE audit_events). brain_privileged no longer runs in the API runtime; it survives only for the deploy-time seed one-shot. The audit append-only and forensic-table REVOKEs were extended to every new role. The runtime-as-role integration test (tests/invariants/integration/db-role-grants.integration.test.ts) applies the real db-roles.sql to a live DB and uses SET ROLE to prove each role both HOLDS its worker's privileges (not too tight) and LACKS the forbidden ones (not too loose); it runs in CI under a superuser DATABASE_URL and skips otherwise.
evidence: infra/db-roles.sql (§4 roles + per-role grant matrix + REVOKEs)
services/api/src/main.ts (per-role pools + per-pool boot role assertions)

risks.title: All background workers run inside the public API process
risks.mitigation_summary: Review 2 flagged that the monolithic main() ran ten background workers (ingestion sync/interpret, canonical + ledger projection, execution-outbox drain, webhook dispatch, blob purge, audit verifier + anchor scheduler/reconciler, agent-route) plus the anchor scheduler inside the same process that serves the public HTTP API, so an api deploy/restart bounced ingestion + execution, horizontal api scaling multiplied workers, and the public api runtime held every worker's privileged DB credential. Closed by one-image, role-via-env separation: BRAIN_HTTP_ENABLED gates the /v1 surface (health always on) and BRAIN_WORKERS (all|none|CSV of nine groups) selects which workers a process runs; composition/process-roles.ts (resolveComposition) is the pure source of truth mapping env -> {/v1, workers, least-privilege role pools}. The db-isolation fence and boot role-assertion only require/check the pools a process actually uses, and an unset role URL aliases brain_app, so an api process (BRAIN_WORKERS=none) holds no worker DB credentials. Defaults (HTTP + all workers) reproduce the historical all-in-one process; docker-compose.prod.yml runs an api service (workers=none) and a worker service (http disabled, workers=all) off the same image. Per-worker advisory leases (shared/src/workers/advisory-lease.ts: leasedCycle wraps each cycle in a per-worker pg_try_advisory_lock) now make the canonical/ledger/normalize/webhook workers multi-replica safe: one replica is active at a time and a crash auto-releases the lock so a standby takes over within one interval. Remaining (deferred to R-03 Azure substrate): per-group autoscaling with genuine per-item parallelism (beyond single-active failover).
evidence: services/api/src/main.ts (gated /v1 block, workers, pools; dynamic shutdown set)
docker-compose.prod.yml (api service + worker service off one image)

risks.title: On-chain executor had only unit/mocked coverage (no testnet E2E)
risks.mitigation_summary: Review 3 (P0 8.1) flagged that the on-chain executor (buildOnchainExecutor + OnchainBaseRail) was only unit/mocked, so 'the on-chain rail works against the deployed contract' was asserted, not proven, and release readiness had no signal. Mitigated by a testnet-gated E2E (tests/e2e/onchain-executor.testnet.e2e.test.ts) that drives the REAL OnchainBaseRail against a deployed BrainSmartAccount on Base Sepolia: read path (nonce, no key/gas), revert path (a real on-chain revert surfaces as execution_rail_declined, gas only), and a double-gated success case (granted-key fixture) that executes once then asserts the on-chain replay guard DIRECTLY: a re-send at the CONSUMED nonce reverts (BadNonce). Review-053af35 P1 corrected this: the prior assertion re-called rail.dispatch, which reads a fresh nonce and would simply do a second transfer, so it did not prove exactly-once; outbox idempotency (same idempotency_key -> one economic effect) is proved separately in the execution outbox suite. An opt-in CI job (.github/workflows/main.yml: testnet_onchain_executor_e2e, gated on vars.TESTNET_ONCHAIN_E2E_ENABLED) runs it; the test self-skips and the job is var-gated so a green check always means a real testnet run. production-readiness reports an 'On-chain executor testnet E2E' row (yellow until the job is wired). Status mitigating not closed: the scaffolding is shipped and ready, but actual execution needs provisioned testnet fixtures (RPC + a deployed smart account + a gas-funded throwaway key; the success case needs a granted session key), which overlaps the deferred R-03 deploy substrate.
